stonetop.summary: Mountain-dwelling young frythancs, twelve-foot wingspans and all.

system.concept: Mountain-dwelling young frythancs, twelve-foot wingspans and all.
system.description: The full-grown frythancs that hunt the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.nBvsH19B1bG2d4Di]{Flats} are terrors. Their young, thankfully, keep to the mountains, where they hunt goats, sheep, and the occasional unwary traveler. The fledglings aren't nearly as dangerous as their full-grown parents — but still, let's see how you do when birds with twelve-foot wingspans try to tear you off some switchback trail.
